title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary:
The reviewer identifies three issues:

1. **`ValidatePolicyRules` will panic** if called with a nil `fldPath` and non-empty rules, because `field.Path.Key()` dereferences a nil pointer. Add a nil guard that defaults `fldPath` to `field.NewPath("rules")`, or document the nil precondition.

2. **`InjectOsloPolicyConfig` mutates its input map in-place**, breaking the package's documented "pure functions" contract where every other function returns a new map. Refactor it to return a new map (or update `doc.go` to drop the "pure functions" claim).

3. **`RenderPastePipeline` silently drops middleware with an unrecognized `Position`** without documenting this or returning an error, unlike other invalid-input cases in the same function. Either return an error for unknown positions or document the silent-drop behavior in the godoc.

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Not addressed: No changes were made to internal/common/policy/policy.go. The nil fldPath panic risk in ValidatePolicyRules remains.

code_comments.body:
**`InjectOsloPolicyConfig` mutates in-place, inconsistent with the rest of the package**

The `config/doc.go` describes this package as providing "pure functions for rendering structured configuration data". Every other function in the package (`RenderINI`, `MergeDefaults`, `InjectSecrets`) returns a new map without touching the input. `InjectOsloPolicyConfig` breaks this contract by mutating `config` in place and returning nothing.

This creates an asymmetric calling pattern:

```go
config = MergeDefaults(userConfig, defaults)   // returns new map
config = InjectSecrets(config, secrets)         // returns new map
InjectOsloPolicyConfig(config, path)            // silently mutates config ŌĆö easy to miss
ini := RenderINI(config)
```

A caller unfamiliar with the implementation who chains these in a different order, or who uses the original `config` map after this call assuming it is unmodified, will see unexpected behaviour.

Consider making it consistent with the rest of the package by returning a new map:

```go
func InjectOsloPolicyConfig(config map[string]map[string]string, policyFilePath string) map[string]map[string]string {
    if policyFilePath == "" {
        return config
    }
    result := MergeDefaults(config, nil)
    if result["oslo_policy"] == nil {
        result["oslo_policy"] = make(map[string]string)
    }
    result["oslo_policy"]["policy_file"] = policyFilePath
    return result
}
```

If in-place mutation is intentional for performance reasons, the `doc.go` description should be updated to remove the "pure functions" characterisation, and the godoc on this function already correctly states "in place" ŌĆö but that single word is easy to overlook.

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown

Path: internal/common/config/config.go
Line: 100-115

Comment:
**`InjectOsloPolicyConfig` mutates in-place, inconsistent with the rest of the package**

The `config/doc.go` describes this package as providing "pure functions for rendering structured configuration data". Every other function in the package (`RenderINI`, `MergeDefaults`, `InjectSecrets`) returns a new map without touching the input. `InjectOsloPolicyConfig` breaks this contract by mutating `config` in place and returning nothing.

This creates an asymmetric calling pattern:

```go
config = MergeDefaults(userConfig, defaults)   // returns new map
config = InjectSecrets(config, secrets)         // returns new map
InjectOsloPolicyConfig(config, path)            // silently mutates config ŌĆö easy to miss
ini := RenderINI(config)
```

A caller unfamiliar with the implementation who chains these in a different order, or who uses the original `config` map after this call assuming it is unmodified, will see unexpected behaviour.

Consider making it consistent with the rest of the package by returning a new map:

```go
func InjectOsloPolicyConfig(config map[string]map[string]string, policyFilePath string) map[string]map[string]string {
    if policyFilePath == "" {
        return config
    }
    result := MergeDefaults(config, nil)
    if result["oslo_policy"] == nil {
        result["oslo_policy"] = make(map[string]string)
    }
    result["oslo_policy"]["policy_file"] = policyFilePath
    return result
}
```

If in-place mutation is intentional for performance reasons, the `doc.go` description should be updated to remove the "pure functions" characterisation, and the godoc on this function already correctly states "in place" ŌĆö but that single word is easy to overlook.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Not addressed: No changes were made to internal/common/config/config.go. InjectOsloPolicyConfig still mutates in-place, inconsistent with the package's pure-function contract.

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Not addressed: No changes were made to internal/common/plugins/plugins.go. The silent drop of unknown-position middleware remains undocumented in the godoc.

resolution_summary: The provided git diff only contains changes to go.work.sum (auto-generated dependency checksum additions), which do not address any of the three review comments. None of the files mentioned in the review feedback (internal/common/policy/policy.go, internal/common/config/config.go, internal/common/plugins/plugins.go) were modified in this diff. The review comments regarding the nil fldPath panic, the in-place mutation inconsistency, and the undocumented silent drop of unknown-position middleware remain unresolved.
